description: Triage classifier eval — MEDIUM. Ambiguous cases, mixed content, short but meaningful chunks.

cases.text: Yeah so basically the API keeps timing out after about thirty seconds.
cases.note: Casual tone but substantive technical issue

cases.text: I don't know, maybe we should just cancel the whole thing.
cases.note: Vague but implies a significant decision

cases.text: She said Tuesday works for her but only after 2 PM.
cases.note: Short but contains actionable scheduling info

cases.text: Wait, that's not what we agreed on last time. The price was supposed to be forty-five, not fifty.
Can you send me the updated spreadsheet when you get a chance?
The password is capital B, lowercase r, zero, four, seven, exclamation mark.
Remind me to call the electrician about the outlet in the kitchen.
That's actually a really good point. I hadn't thought about the tax implications.
cases.note: Acknowledgment that advances a substantive discussion

cases.text: We're hemorrhaging money on that account. Something like ten thousand a month.
cases.note: Casual language but critical financial info

cases.text: I think the interview went well. They said they'd get back to me by end of week.
No no no, that's the wrong version. We need the one from March, not February.
cases.note: Sounds like filler but contains important correction

cases.text: Okay let's table that for now and circle back after lunch.
cases.note: Meeting management but still meaningful context

cases.text: He mentioned something about the warranty being voided if we open it ourselves.
cases.note: Casual hearsay but contains important constraint

cases.text: The tests are passing locally but failing in CI. Might be an environment issue.
cases.note: Speculative but identifies a real problem

cases.text: Actually, I just remembered — the deposit is due by the 15th, not the 30th.
cases.note: Casual recall but contains a critical date correction

cases.text: I forwarded you the email from the accountant. Take a look when you can.
The pediatrician wants to see her again in three months for the allergy follow-up.
They're asking for a 30-day extension on the deliverable. Should we grant it?
I noticed the error rate spiked around 2 AM. Could be related to the cron job.
The landlord agreed to fix the AC but only if we extend the lease by a year.
Make sure we back up the database before running the migration script.
We got three new support tickets about the same login issue today.
The insurance company denied the claim. We need to appeal by next Friday.
I think we should A/B test the new checkout flow before rolling it out to everyone.
Just a heads up, the parking garage will be closed next week for maintenance.
Oh by the way, your prescription is ready for pickup at CVS.
cases.note: Casual delivery of actionable personal info

cases.text: The app is crashing for users on Android 12. It works fine on everything else.
We probably shouldn't merge that PR until we get sign-off from security.
The babysitter can't make it Saturday. We need to find someone else.
I heard they're laying off 20% of the engineering team next quarter.
The demo is at 3 PM Pacific, not Eastern. Double-check your calendar.
We should probably add two-factor authentication before the security audit.
She's not going to renew. She told me she's going with the competitor.
cases.note: Short but critical client retention info

cases.text: The load balancer is routing all traffic to a single instance. That's why it's slow.
Can you pick up the dry cleaning on your way home? It closes at 7.
The prototype passed all the safety tests. We're clear to move to production.
I'm going to push back on the timeline. Two weeks is not enough for this scope.
The tenant complained about the water heater again. Third time this month.
We need to renew the domain name before it expires on the 22nd.
The supplier is raising prices by 8% starting next quarter.
Can you double-check the invoice total? Something doesn't add up.
He said the earliest available appointment is three weeks out.
The staging environment is down again. Can someone restart the Docker containers?
I think we need to revisit the pricing strategy. We're leaving money on the table.
The nanny is asking for a raise from 25 to 30 an hour.
Just FYI, the elevator will be out of service all day Thursday for maintenance.
The new intern accidentally deleted the production database. We're restoring from backup.
We should send a thank-you note to the client after the presentation.
The estimate came in at $45,000 for the roof replacement.
Let's move the standup to 9:30 instead of 9 so the West Coast team can join.
Yeah it's been a long week. I'm so tired. Same here, can't wait for the weekend.
Sorry what? Can you repeat that? The connection is bad.
Oh cool. Nice. That's awesome. Yeah totally.
I mean, I guess, like, whatever works I suppose.
Hey did you see that thing on Twitter? No what? Oh never mind it's not important.
cases.note: Starts as potential info but self-dismisses

cases.text: Hold on, someone's at the door. Be right back.
Sorry I'm late, parking was a nightmare.
You're breaking up. Can you hear me now? Hello? Is this better?
Alright guys, I think that's about it. Good talk. See everyone next week.
Let me share my screen. Can everyone see it? Give me a thumbs up. Okay great.
Oh gosh, excuse me. Sorry about that. Okay, where was I?
You look great by the way! New haircut? Yeah, got it done yesterday.
I need more coffee. Anyone want anything from the kitchen? I'm good, thanks.
My back is killing me. I need a better chair. Yeah, these office chairs are terrible.
What time is it? Almost three. Wow, time flies.
Oh shoot, I'm on mute. Can you hear me now? Yes, we can hear you.
Same old same old. You know how it is. Yeah, I hear you.
Okay who just joined? Oh hey Mark, we just started.
Wait, is this being recorded? Oh, I don't think so. Okay good.
Ha, classic. That's so typical. Of course that happened.
Let me just close the door real quick. Okay, sorry about that.
Okay I need to step away for a sec. Be right back.
Oh I love that song. Who sings this? I think it's The Weeknd.
Did you see the sunset yesterday? It was gorgeous. I missed it.
Oh no, I think I left my lunch in the car. Do you want to split mine?
Is it just me or does this room smell weird? Yeah, it's the carpet. They just cleaned it.
Oh hey, I like your new glasses. Thanks, I just got them last week.
Sorry, my cat just jumped on my keyboard. Can you see her? Oh she's cute!
Are we waiting for anyone else? I think Lisa is running late.
I can barely keep my eyes open. Should have gone to bed earlier. Same.
Oh man, I just realized it's daylight savings this weekend. Ugh, I always forget.
Let me switch to my headphones real quick. Can you still hear me? Yep, sounds good.
Bless you! Sorry, the dust in here is crazy. Yeah, they need to clean the vents.
I think we lost Dave. His screen is frozen. Dave, are you there?
Okay everyone, let's give it another minute for people to join.
Your background is so cool. Where is that? Oh, it's just a virtual background.
Ha, your kid just walked in behind you. That was adorable.
Is that a new keyboard? It sounds so clicky. Yeah, it's a mechanical one.
Okay sorry about the noise. My neighbor is doing construction. It's been going on all week.
Oh wait, wrong screen. Let me switch. There we go. Can you see it now?
Yeah yeah, exactly, for sure, absolutely, a hundred percent.
I'm going to grab some water. Does anyone need anything? No? Okay.
Oh man, did you see that email from corporate? The one about the dress code? So ridiculous.
My internet has been so spotty today. Sorry if I drop off. No worries.
Okay, one sec, my headset is dying. Let me plug it in. Alright, I'm good.
Wow, your office looks so organized. Mine is a disaster right now.
Happy birthday by the way! Oh thanks! Doing anything fun? Just dinner with the family.
Okay, are we all set? Thumbs up? Great. Let's dive in.
Oh, I think I hear an echo. Is anyone hearing that? Let me try muting and unmuting.
Okay I think that's everything. Thanks everyone. Have a great rest of your day!
